title: Tigrul de hârtie din jungla unei lumi multipolare
summary: The article argues that NATO has become a 'paper tiger' as Trump himself declared, and that Europe must shift its paradigm to view the US as a geopolitical rival rather than a protector. The author contends that whether Europe intervenes in the Strait of Hormuz crisis or not, it must do so for the right reasons—building autonomous security, energy, and military capabilities rather than clinging to an outdated transatlantic framework.
talking_points: NATO was always transactional—the US provided security in exchange for political solidarity and recognition of American geopolitical supremacy, but this strategic convergence no longer exists
Europe must operate in a new paradigm where the US is a geopolitical rival alongside China and Russia, not a paternalistic protector
Any European intervention in the Strait of Hormuz should be motivated by protecting economic interests and building autonomous capabilities, not by preserving an obsolete security framework
The EU is already building its multipolar position through trade agreements with MERCOSUR, India, and Australia, and discussions about UK rejoining the EU signal a new direction
Europe should continue supporting Ukraine and integrate it into a new European security architecture where Russia is a manageable threat and NATO is optional
author: Andrei Colțea
